topic: Cardiac embryology, fetal circulation, and congenital heart defects
notes: Heart morphogenesis, fetal/postnatal circulation, congenital heart defects (5 T's; VSD/ASD/PDA, Eisenmenger), coarctation of aorta, persistent pHTN of newborn, defect-syndrome associations. UWorld questions.

topic: Anatomy: chambers, surface anatomy, CT/CXR correlates, and pericardium
Physiology: Cardiac output (variables + equations); cardiac and vascular function curves; pressure-volume loops and the cardiac cycle
Heart sounds and splitting; murmurs and the maneuvers that change their intensity
notes: Splitting of S2, full auscultation map, systolic and diastolic murmurs, maneuvers. UWorld questions.

topic: Cardiac action potentials; ECG (narrow- and wide-complex arrhythmias, conduction blocks); BP control, autoregulation, and Starling forces
notes: Myocyte + pacemaker APs, ECG, baroreceptor and chemoreceptor reflexes, normal cardiac pressures, autoregulation by organ, capillary fluid exchange (Starling), atrial fib/flutter, ventricular arrhythmias, torsades, conduction blocks. UWorld questions.

topic: Coronary vessels, coronary artery disease, ischemic heart disease, evolution of MI, and MI complications
notes: CAD, IHD manifestations, MI evolution and histology, biomarkers, ECG localisation by lead, MI complications. UWorld questions.

topic: Hypertension, hyperlipidemia, atherosclerosis, aortic aneurysms, and aortic dissection
notes: Hyperlipidemia signs, atherosclerosis pathogenesis, arteriolosclerosis (hyaline + hyperplastic), abdominal and thoracic aortic aneurysms, aortic dissection (Stanford A vs B), subclavian steal. UWorld questions.

topic: Cardiomyopathies, heart failure, shock, and sudden cardiac death
notes: DCM, HCM, RCM, takotsubo, peripartum CMP. Left and right heart failure (HFrEF / HFpEF). Shock (hypovolemic, cardiogenic, obstructive, distributive). Sudden cardiac death and syncope. UWorld questions.

topic: Rheumatic fever, infective endocarditis, pericardial disease, myocarditis, cardiac tumors, and vasculitis
notes: Acute rheumatic fever, rheumatic heart disease, infective endocarditis (acute, subacute, Libman-Sacks, marantic), acute pericarditis + tamponade, myocarditis, cardiac tumors (myxoma, rhabdomyoma, mets), large/medium/small vessel vasculitides, vascular tumors. UWorld questions.

topic: Cardiovascular pharmacology — antihypertensives, antianginals, lipid-lowering, antiarrhythmics, and heart-failure agents
notes: Hypertension treatment by comorbidity, ACE inhibitors / ARBs, β-blockers, calcium channel blockers (CCBs), hydralazine, ranolazine, sacubitril/valsartan, lipid-lowering agents (statins, fibrates, niacin, ezetimibe, PCSK9, BAS), digoxin, antiarrhythmics (Class I A/B/C, II, III, IV, adenosine, magnesium), pulmonary hypertension drugs. UWorld questions.

topic: Respiratory embryology — lung development, alveolar cells, surfactant, branching, congenital anomalies
notes: Lung development phases (embryonic, pseudoglandular, canalicular, saccular, alveolar), bronchial branching, alveolar cell types (Type I/II, club cells), surfactant, congenital anomalies (TE fistula, pulmonary hypoplasia). UWorld questions.

topic: Respiratory anatomy — respiratory tree, bronchopulmonary segments, diaphragm, pleura
notes: Conducting vs respiratory zones, bronchopulmonary segments, lung relations, diaphragm + accessory muscles of breathing, pleural anatomy. UWorld questions.

topic: Physiology I — lung volumes, mechanics, compliance, surface tension, O₂–Hb dissociation
notes: TLC/FRC/VC/RV, important pulmonary equations, lung-chest wall mechanics, compliance and hysteresis, surface tension and Laplace's law, oxygen-hemoglobin dissociation curve, methemoglobin and carboxyhemoglobin. UWorld questions.

topic: Physiology II — A–a gradient, V/Q mismatch, pulmonary circulation, high altitude
notes: O₂ content equation, A–a gradient, oxygen delivery, V/Q ratios across the lung and how shunt vs dead space behave, pulmonary circulation, hypoxic pulmonary vasoconstriction, response to high altitude. UWorld questions.

topic: Physiology III — control of breathing, response to exercise / hypoxia / hypercapnia, sleep apnea, breathing patterns
notes: Central + peripheral chemoreceptors, ventilation control, response to exercise, hypoxia, hypercapnia. Breathing patterns (Cheyne-Stokes, Kussmaul, ataxic). Sleep apnea physiology. UWorld questions.

topic: Pathology I — upper airway and obstructive lung disease
notes: Rhinosinusitis, deviated septum, epistaxis, head and neck cancer, sleep apnea (obstructive vs central), asthma, COPD (emphysema and chronic bronchitis), bronchiectasis. UWorld questions.

topic: Pathology II — restrictive lung disease, ARDS, pulmonary vascular, pneumonia
notes: Restrictive lung disease (intrinsic vs extrinsic), idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, sarcoidosis, pneumoconioses, hypersensitivity pneumonitis, ARDS, neonatal RDS, pulmonary embolism, pulmonary edema, pulmonary hypertension. Pneumonia overview (lobar, bronchopneumonia, interstitial), specific pneumonias by organism, lung abscess. UWorld questions.

topic: Pathology III + Pharmacology — TB, fungal, pleural, mediastinal, lung cancer; respiratory drugs
notes: Tuberculosis (primary, secondary, miliary, latent), fungal pneumonias, pleural diseases (pneumothorax, effusion, mesothelioma, hemothorax), mediastinal masses, lung cancer (small cell vs non-small cell, paraneoplastic syndromes, Pancoast tumor). Respiratory pharmacology — asthma drugs (β2 agonists, ICS, LTRAs, biologics), COPD drugs, methylxanthines, antitussives, decongestants, antihistamines. UWorld questions.
